title: triticum sativum subsp. dicoccum
intro: Triticum sativum subsp. dicoccum, also known as emmer wheat, is an ancient subspecies of wheat that is characterized by its distinctive grain shape and hard outer layer. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The emmer wheat has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing cholesterol levels, improving digestion, and supporting bone health. Emmer wheat can be prepared as an herbal tea, porridge, or flour for use in baking, making it a versatile and nutritious addition to a healthy diet.
